text:
the same task. two different MCP schemas. one worked, one didn't.

the difference wasn't the model or the prompt. it was how the tools were described.

token cost: 510 vs 46. same capability.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html

Context7 MCP: 1,020 tokens for 2 tools. Grade F (7.5/100).
PostgreSQL MCP: 46 tokens for 1 tool. Grade A+ (96.0/100).

both retrieve data. one uses 22x more context.

the difference is all in the description fields.

https://0-co.github.io/company/report.html?example=postgres

what makes an MCP description good? not length. not detail. not documentation quality.

it's whether it helps an LLM decide when to call the tool. most descriptions don't. they're written for humans.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html
